Bug fix · classified auto · confidence 0.95

Goal Card leaks raw markdown onto the zero-click surface

Stop the Goal Card from printing literal markdown syntax — bold, backtick-code, and emphasis — so the one above-the-fold surface a first-time reader sees reads as clean prose instead of source.

2
leaking emit sites
Outcome: the leak is closed at both emit sites by reusing the existing inline-markdown renderer — no new renderer added. Scope: surgical, two emit sites in renderer.py fed by goal_card.py. The sibling _first_sentence truncation defect is out of scope (tracked separately).

The problem

What a first reader sees today — and why it's wrong

The refined-requirements render has one job above all others: a reader who opens a goal grasps the job, the outcome, and the scope in seconds, from the Goal Card alone. Right now the card betrays that job by printing raw markdown syntax onto the zero-click surface.

First impression a reader actually gets
Replace the **plain** deterministic render
  • The leak lives in two emit sites in renderer.py, both fed by the pure heuristics in goal_card.py: the job statement (_render_goal_card builds job_html through escape() only) and the L2 assertions (_render_assertions emits each through escape() with a "no markdown wrapper" comment).
  • escape() HTML-escapes but never interprets markdown, so any inline markdown an author wrote in the Intent or Success-Criteria cells survives to the screen as raw syntax.
  • The defect is data-dependent — a source with no inline markdown looks fine — which is exactly why it slipped past the golden-HTML snapshot until an author used emphasis.

The fix

Both Goal Card emit sites resolve inline markdown

Route both emit sites through the existing inline-markdown path the recipe sections already use, so emphasis, code, and bold resolve to formatted HTML instead of literal syntax — reusing the existing renderer rather than adding a new one.

Emit site 1 — the job statement
FR-001

The Goal Card job statement is rendered through the same inline-markdown path the recipe sections already use (_md_to_html), so **bold**, `code`, and _emphasis_ resolve to formatted HTML instead of literal syntax.

Reuse the existing shared markdown instance; do not add a second renderer.

Emit site 2 — the L2 assertions
FR-002

Each L2 assertion on the Goal Card resolves inline markdown the same way, while staying a single contiguous <li> with no fragmenting spans.

The "no markdown wrapper" comment is the bug, not the contract.

Guardrails

What the fix must not cross

The change is invisible everywhere except the leak itself: it preserves the thin-spine DOM the comment layer anchors to, and a markdown-free source still renders byte-for-byte what it does today.

DOM contract
FR-003

The fix introduces no element id= and no data-block-anchor anywhere on the Goal Card, preserving the thin-spine DOM contract the comment-and-version layer anchors against.

A markdown renderer that emits header ids would violate this — inline-only conversion is required.

No regression
FR-004

A render whose source contains no inline markdown produces byte-identical Goal Card output to today, so the change is invisible except where it fixes the leak.

Protects the existing golden snapshots for markdown-free fixtures.

Verification

How we'll know it's fixed

The fix is proven at three points — where it changes behavior, where it must not touch the DOM contract, and where it must stay byte-identical — each pairing one criterion to its verification method.

SC-001

The Goal Card of a source containing **bold**, `code`, and _emphasis_ renders formatted text with zero literal markdown characters visible.

How verified

Render a fixture carrying inline markdown; assert no stray * / ` / _ in the Goal Card HTML text.

SC-002

No id= and no data-block-anchor appear anywhere in the regenerated Goal Card markup.

How verified

Grep the rendered Goal Card section.

SC-003

A markdown-free source renders a byte-identical Goal Card to the pre-fix output.

How verified

Golden-snapshot diff on an emphasis-free fixture.

Out of scope: the sibling _first_sentence abbreviation-truncation defect (splits on sentence boundaries and clips job statements containing e.g. or v2.). Different root cause, different test — tracked separately to keep this a one-file fix.
Refined requirements · goal e2e-bug-fix · family: bug fix
